answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Bubble Sort is recognized by adjacent swaps. In each pass, the largest unsorted value moves toward the end of the array.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Look for the right side becoming fixed after every pass.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Selection Sort repeatedly finds the minimum element in the unsorted part and swaps it with the current front position.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Look for the smallest remaining value moving to the left side each pass.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Insertion Sort builds a sorted left part. Each new element is inserted into its correct position by shifting larger elements.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Look for the sorted prefix growing one element at a time.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: This trace is Bubble Sort because the largest elements move to the right and become fixed after each pass.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Largest value moving to the end is the strongest clue.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Bubble Sort uses adjacent swaps. Selection Sort selects the minimum and places it at the front. Insertion Sort grows a sorted left section and is usually best for nearly sorted arrays.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Bubble: swaps. Selection: minimum. Insertion: sorted prefix.
